name: Mardi Gras Meringue Crepes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small mixer bowl, beat egg whites with cream of tartar at high speed until foamy.
2. Add granulated sugar, 2 tablespoons at a time, beating constantly until sugar is dissolved (to test if sugar has been dissolved, rub just a bit of meringue between thumb and forefinger) and whites are glossy and stand in soft peaks.
3. Beat in rum extract; gently fold in pecans.
4. Spread about 1/3 cup meringue across half of each crepe.
5. Starting at the midpoint of the edge of the filled side, roll up the crepe, pressing lightly so filling puffs out at the ends of the rolled crepe.
6. Arrange filled crepes in a greased 13x9x2-inch baking dish; bake in a preheated 475°F oven until meringue is firm but not brown, about 7 to 9 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, in a small saucepan over medium heat, bring brown sugar, water, and ginger to a boil.
8. Boil and stir the syrup until thickened, about 4 minutes.
9. Gently stir in peaches; cook until heated through, about 1 to 2 minutes.
10. Serve crepes hot with peach syrup.
CREPES
In a mixer bowl, combine egg yolks, milk, water, and 3 tablespoons melted butter; beat until well blended.
Add flour and salt; beat until smooth.
Heat a 10-inch omelet or crepe pan over medium-high heat until just hot enough to sizzle a drop of water. Brush the pan lightly with melted butter.
For each crepe, pour in 1/4 cup batter, tipping and tilting the pan to move the batter quickly over the bottom. Cook until lightly browned on the bottom and dry on top. Remove the crepe from the pan or, if desired, turn and brown the other side. Brush the pan lightly with melted butter as needed to prevent sticking. Stack crepes between layers of wax paper until ready to fill.
